repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue entry studies randomized-saturation variance reduction; closest API entries are Q1 panel interference regression decompositions, not saturation-law design optimization.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 derives closed-form bias and variance under linear interference; this lemma is a narrowed standalone specialization.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_saturation_skew_threshold archived angle0 failed by dropping the -V_4 m_2(nu)^2 term; no accepted in-catalogue theorem gives this profiled quartic certificate.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 optimizes structured randomized/deterministic families; Winkler1988 and Pinelis2012 give generic moment-set geometry but not the saturation-design global certificate.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_saturation_skew_threshold archived reviews already identified support-at-most-three as surviving supporting machinery.

published_prior_art.cite: Winkler1988 and Pinelis2012 essentially supply the moment-slice finite-atomic reduction after profiling on s=m_2(nu), but not as a saturation-design corollary.
CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 shows endpoint optimality can fail under interference but does not give this global iff threshold for delta_pbar.
CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 does not exhibit this algebraic feasible skewed law beating the stratified benchmark in the quartic objective.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_saturation_skew_threshold archived angle1 already targeted exact-compatible finite implementation, though not this repaired global-certificate package.

published_prior_art.cite: BasseFeller2016 develops finite two-stage analysis and CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 passes to asymptotics, but neither states the separate mass/grid denominator realization theorem.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_saturation_skew_threshold archived angle1 contained a related rounding-loss target, but required a repaired split-grid construction.

published_prior_art.cite: BasseFeller2016 and CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 do not provide an O(M^(-1)+m^(-1)) optimizer-preserving rounding certificate for a continuous quartic optimum.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_saturation_skew_threshold archived angle0 already included the SUTVA-corner sanity result.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 already proves the SUTVA endpoint comparison; this is a useful reduction plus finite-integrality split.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry maps the full coefficient space for the quartic saturation objective.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 treats structured design families; no verified paper gives the full semialgebraic optimizer partition.

soundness_flags.label: thm:global-certificate
soundness_flags.one_line: The certificate compares G_{a,c}(s), which omits the constant V_0, directly to V(nu_star); the statement must either normalize the objective by V_0 or include V_0 in the outer certificate.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:homogeneous-linear-share-working-model / lem:variance-moment-reduction
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Cai-style variance moment expansion is treated as inherited working-model setup rather than as the novelty contribution.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The optimization claims are conditional on the specialized homogeneous linear-in-share variance representation V(nu)=V_0+V_1 m_2+V_3 m_3+V_4(m_4-m_2^2).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair the global certificate by defining the dual lower bound for V(nu)-V_0 or by adding V_0 to G_{a,c}, then propagate that normalization through the positive-penalty threshold and branch-map handle.

Demote support-at-most-three to explicitly cited supporting machinery from Winkler/Pinelis and keep the paper's novelty focused on the saturation-specific certificate, threshold, witness, and implementation theorems.
Add a worked small finite design exhibit for the skewed witness, including concrete M,m satisfying the mass and within-cluster grid constraints, to make finite implementability inspectable.
Add a boundary corollary for pbar=1/2 versus pbar!=1/2 in the positive-penalty branch so the repaired theorem visibly avoids the earlier archived V4 endpoint mistake.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: fix the V_0 normalization in the global certificate, then advance to Stage 0 if no new algebraic defect appears.
